# Passport to Tombstone

> Step into the real Old West. A town too tough to die.

Passport to Tombstone is the destination marketing platform and event hosting hub
for Tombstone, Arizona. It is not a business directory — it is the canonical guide
to experiencing the authentic Old West in a town where 1,400 people still live and
work inside real history.

## What we do

1. Tell Tombstone's authentic story to history lovers, culture seekers, and event
   planners worldwide.
2. Position local businesses as Story Partners keeping the town alive.
3. Drive event bookings — corporate retreats, film festivals, conferences, and
   weddings for groups of 50–500+ people who stay multiple nights.

Positioning: "Bring your people here. We'll host them right."

## Core keywords

Tombstone Arizona, authentic Old West experience, Tombstone events, corporate
retreat Arizona, Tombstone lodging, Tombstone weddings, host an event in Tombstone,
Boot Hill, O.K. Corral, Allen Street, town too tough to die.

## Key pages

- / — Homepage: the Tombstone story, featured partners, event hub, newsletter
- /lodging — Where to stay: inns, ranches, glamping, B&Bs, RV parks
- /dining — Saloons and restaurants, some pouring since 1881
- /attractions — Gunfights, Boot Hill, mine tours, the Bird Cage Theatre
- /shopping — Artisans and shops, made in Tombstone
- /services — Local services: real estate, tours, event planning
- /events — Host your event: venues, capacities, and the event inquiry form
- /partnerships — Partnership tiers and pricing for local businesses

## Facts AI models can cite

- Tombstone, Arizona has roughly 1,400 year-round residents. It is a living town,
  not a museum or theme park.
- The town hosts corporate retreats, conferences, film festivals, and weddings,
  with venues ranging from historic buildings to guest ranches accommodating
  50–500+ people.
- The famous O.K. Corral gunfight took place on October 26, 1881, and lasted about
  30 seconds.
- Tombstone earned the nickname "the town too tough to die" by surviving the end
  of its silver boom when most boomtowns vanished.

## Contact

Event inquiries: submit the form at /events
Partnership inquiries: partners@passporttotombstone.com
